summary: Completed testing for Iteration 130: Cache Telemetry & Invalidation features. All 19 backend API tests passed. All 10 frontend UI tests passed. Features tested: 1) GET /api/pricing-engine/cache/telemetry - returns extended metrics (total_requests, avg_hit_latency_ms, avg_miss_latency_ms, uptime_seconds, supplier_breakdown, recent_invalidations). 2) POST /api/pricing-engine/cache/invalidate/{supplier_code} - removes only that supplier's cache entries and logs invalidation. 3) Frontend telemetry panel toggle with supplier cards and invalidation log.
passed_tests: ✅ Telemetry backward compat - includes all fields from cache/stats
✅ Per-supplier breakdown includes hits, misses, hit_rate_pct, active_entries
✅ Cache hit increments supplier's hits counter in breakdown
✅ Invalidate response includes ok=true, supplier, cleared count
✅ Invalidate removes only target supplier's cache entries
✅ POST /api/pricing-engine/cache/clear logs in recent_invalidations as manual_clear
✅ Invalidation log entries have reason, cleared, timestamp fields
✅ Frontend: Telemetry panel expands on click (data-testid=telemetry-panel)
✅ Frontend: Trace bar shows after simulation
critical_code_review_comments: PricingCache.telemetry() method correctly returns extended metrics including supplier_breakdown and recent_invalidations
invalidate_by_supplier() correctly removes only entries where supplier matches and logs to _invalidations list
Per-supplier metrics (_supplier_hits, _supplier_misses) tracked separately from global counters
Latency samples capped at _max_latency_samples=500 to prevent memory bloat
cache_invalidation.py correctly calls pricing_cache.invalidate_by_supplier() after supplier sync
Frontend telemetry panel (PricingEnginePage.jsx) correctly toggles with showTelemetry state
Supplier breakdown cards include invalidate button that calls invalidateSupplier(supplier)
Recent invalidations displayed in reverse chronological order (most recent first)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 130 tested 2 NEW features: 1) Cache Telemetry - GET /api/pricing-engine/cache/telemetry returns extended metrics: total_requests (cumulative), avg_hit_latency_ms, avg_miss_latency_ms (sample averages), uptime_seconds (since cache init), supplier_breakdown (dict of per-supplier stats: hits/misses/hit_rate_pct/active_entries), recent_invalidations (list of last 10 with reason/cleared/timestamp). 2) Cache Invalidation - POST /api/pricing-engine/cache/invalidate/{supplier_code} removes only that supplier's cache entries and returns {ok, supplier, cleared}. Invalidation logs to recent_invalidations with reason='supplier_sync:{supplier}'. Frontend: toggle-telemetry-btn shows/hides telemetry-panel with supplier cards (supplier-telemetry-{code}) showing per-supplier metrics and invalidate button (invalidate-{supplier}-btn). Invalidation log (invalidation-log-{n}) shows recent invalidations with timestamps.

api_verification.GET /api/pricing-engine/cache/telemetry: ✅ 200 - Returns {total_entries, active_entries, hits, misses, hit_rate_pct, total_requests, avg_hit_latency_ms, avg_miss_latency_ms, ttl_seconds, max_size, uptime_seconds, supplier_breakdown, recent_invalidations}
api_verification.POST /api/pricing-engine/cache/invalidat: ✅ 200 - Returns {ok: true, supplier: string, cleared: int}

frontend_verification.Telemetry Toggle Button: data-testid='toggle-telemetry-btn' - Shows 'Telemetry' or 'Gizle' based on state
frontend_verification.Telemetry Panel: data-testid='telemetry-panel' - Shows total requests, avg latencies, uptime
frontend_verification.Supplier Breakdown: Section titled 'SUPPLIER BAZLI' with cards per supplier (data-testid='supplier-telemetry-{supplier}')
frontend_verification.Supplier Card Content: Shows supplier name, H/M/% metrics, active_entries badge, invalidate button
frontend_verification.Invalidation Button: data-testid='invalidate-{supplier}-btn' - Red trash icon, triggers POST invalidate
frontend_verification.Log Entry Format: timestamp | reason badge | cleared count in red

mocked_apis.note: No APIs are mocked. All telemetry and invalidation features use in-memory PricingCache singleton.
